candidate_problems.text: Identify measurable latent state variables—trust, legitimacy, gang-network connectivity, institutional learning, youth opportunity—that best predict basin shifts between high- and low-violence regimes.
Test whether reducing the effective reproduction number of serious violence below 1 across key subnetworks predicts durable citywide safety gains better than conventional crime-rate metrics.
Estimate how specific interventions alter the Jacobian of the urban safety system rather than merely suppress incidents temporarily.
Determine why apparently comparable cities occupy different hidden coordinates despite similar observed crime levels, and whether those coordinates can be shifted directly.
